title: crotalaria novae-hollandiae subsp. lasiophylla
intro: Crotalaria novae-hollandiae subsp. lasiophylla, also known as the Australian rattle pod or rattle box, is a plant species native to Australia, characterized by its distinctive rattle-like seed pods. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Crotalaria novae-hollandiae subsp. lasiophylla include the potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit immunomodulatory effects. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made from its leaves and seeds, and can be found in the form of teas, tinctures, and infusions.
